description: Basic PUBLISH message with application payload

autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

# Test framework provides 'msg' with deserialized message
assert isinstance(msg, Publish)
assert msg.request == 239714735
assert msg.topic == 'com.myapp.mytopic1'
assert msg.args == ['Hello, world!']
assert msg.kwargs is None or msg.kwargs == {}

# Check that options are empty/default (all None)
assert msg.acknowledge is None
assert msg.exclude_me is None
assert msg.retain is None

construction.autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

msg = Publish(
    request=239714735,
    topic='com.myapp.mytopic1',
    args=['Hello, world!']
)

autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

# Test framework provides 'msg' with deserialized message
assert isinstance(msg, Publish)
assert msg.request == 123456789
assert msg.topic == 'com.myapp.encrypted'

# Transparent payload mode: args/kwargs are None, payload is bytes
assert msg.args is None
assert msg.kwargs is None
assert msg.payload is not None
assert isinstance(msg.payload, bytes)
assert msg.payload.hex() == '82a5636f6c6f72a66f72616e6765a573697a65739317002a07'

# E2E encryption metadata in options
assert msg.enc_algo == 'cryptobox'
assert msg.enc_serializer == 'msgpack'

construction.autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

# Simulated encrypted payload (opaque bytes)
payload_bytes = bytes.fromhex('82a5636f6c6f72a66f72616e6765a573697a65739317002a07')

msg = Publish(
    request=123456789,
    topic='com.myapp.encrypted',
    payload=payload_bytes,
    enc_algo='cryptobox',
    enc_serializer='msgpack'
)

autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

# Test framework provides 'msg' with deserialized message
assert isinstance(msg, Publish)
assert msg.request == 987654321
assert msg.topic == 'com.myapp.topic2'
assert msg.args == [42, 'test']
assert msg.kwargs is None or msg.kwargs == {}

# Check forward_for (router-to-router link)
assert msg.forward_for is not None
assert isinstance(msg.forward_for, list)
assert len(msg.forward_for) == 1

ff = msg.forward_for[0]
assert isinstance(ff, dict)
assert ff['session'] == 3456789012
assert ff['authid'] == 'client1'
assert ff['authrole'] == 'frontend'

# Normal payload mode (not E2EE)
assert msg.payload is None
assert msg.enc_algo is None
assert msg.enc_serializer is None

samples.description: PUBLISH with both forward_for and E2EE (advanced R-Link + encryption)

autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

# Test framework provides 'msg' with deserialized message
assert isinstance(msg, Publish)
assert msg.request == 555666777
assert msg.topic == 'com.myapp.secure'

# Transparent payload mode (E2EE)
assert msg.args is None
assert msg.kwargs is None
assert msg.payload is not None
assert isinstance(msg.payload, bytes)
assert msg.payload.hex() == '81a56e6f74696365b1726f757465723120666f72776172646564'

# E2EE metadata
assert msg.enc_algo == 'cryptobox'
assert msg.enc_serializer == 'cbor'

# Router-to-router forwarding
assert msg.forward_for is not None
assert isinstance(msg.forward_for, list)
assert len(msg.forward_for) == 1

ff = msg.forward_for[0]
assert isinstance(ff, dict)
assert ff['session'] == 8901234567
assert ff['authid'] == 'router1'
assert ff['authrole'] == 'router'

construction.autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

# Simulated encrypted payload forwarded from another router
payload_bytes = bytes.fromhex('81a56e6f74696365b1726f757465723120666f72776172646564')

msg = Publish(
    request=555666777,
    topic='com.myapp.secure',
    payload=payload_bytes,
    enc_algo='cryptobox',
    enc_serializer='cbor',
    forward_for=[
        {
            'session': 8901234567,
            'authid': 'router1',
            'authrole': 'router'
        }
    ]
)

notes: Sample 1: Basic PUBLISH with normal payload mode (args/kwargs)
Sample 2: PUBLISH with transparent payload mode (E2E encryption)
Sample 3: PUBLISH with forward_for (router-to-router link, normal payload)
Sample 4: PUBLISH with both forward_for and E2EE (most advanced deployment)
ATTRIBUTE NAMING: Autobahn Implementation vs WAMP Spec 14.1 (Draft)
│ Autobahn:          │ WAMP Spec 14.1:    │
│ enc_algo           │ ppt_scheme         │
│ enc_serializer     │ ppt_serializer     │
│ enc_key            │ ppt_keyid          │
│ (N/A)              │ ppt_cipher         │
NOTE: WAMP spec section 14.1 (Payload Transparency) text not yet finalized.
Test vectors use Autobahn implementation attribute names above.
See: https://github.com/wamp-proto/wamp-proto/blob/master/WIP_E2EE_AND_RLINKS.md
Valid enc_algo values: 'null', 'cryptobox', 'mqtt', 'xbr'
Valid enc_serializer values: 'null', 'json', 'msgpack', 'cbor', 'ubjson', 'opaque', 'flatbuffers'
ppt_cipher is hardcoded for 'cryptobox': xsalsa20poly1305 or aes256gcm
Normal mode: Application payload in args/kwargs (router deserializes)
Transparent mode: Application payload as opaque bytes (router does NOT deserialize)
Transparent mode enables E2E encryption: router cannot read encrypted payload
enc_algo and enc_serializer in Options indicate encryption/serialization used
Payload bytes must be preserved exactly through router (byte-for-byte)
forward_for: Router-to-router link forwarding chain
- List of dicts tracking message forwarding across multiple routers
- Each dict has: session (int), authid (str), authrole (str)
- Enables distributed WAMP router fabrics (R-Links)
- Can be combined with E2EE for encrypted multi-router deployments
Serializers list contains valid byte representations (at least one must match)
Validation blocks are executable code (at least one must pass)
Construction creates a message instance for serialization tests
